tasks_completed.description: Iteration 2: Remove inline bash invocation from commands/clean.md line 589; replace with slash-command-only direction (/commit then /push)
tasks_completed.changes: Line 589: removed 'run bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly' (inline bash invocation violating AC1). New text: 'If satisfied: Changes already committed — to publish to remote: run `/commit` (to register the commit and write the push token), then `/push`'
tasks_completed.rationale: QA iteration 1 rejection: line 589 included an inline bash invocation ('bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly') as primary workaround, violating BA spec AC1 requirement for 'direction toward next command (slash command), not inline git invocations'. The /commit then /push slash-command chain is the correct resolution per orchestrator's accepted end-state.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — architectural gap, not a regression
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: clean.md was authored with a different architecture (cleaner commits inline, no dev-cycle artifact chain). The /push ending-workflow pattern from dev.md was never propagated to clean.md or other file-modifying commands outside the dev-cycle architecture.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Adds the missing next-step direction to /push in Step 17's Next Steps block, mirroring dev.md Step 15's 'complete work path' branch pattern. Also documents the push-gate workaround since the cleaner's inline commit does not produce a push-gate token.

dev.scope_review_reason: Codex identified that /push requires a push-gate token which the cleaner subagent does not write. The current wording includes `bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh` as a workaround — this is an inline bash invocation, which the BA spec said to avoid ('direction toward next command, not inline git invocations'). The correct resolution is a separate dev cycle to have the cleaner subagent write the push-gate token after its inline commit. Until then, the workaround wording is the most honest guidance available.
dev.qa_notes: Verify: (1) /push appears in commands/clean.md line ~589 Step 17 next-step block. (2) /close does NOT appear as a required step in that block. (3) The existing cleaner subagent commit behavior in agents/cleaner.md is unchanged. (4) The enumeration table below matches the BA spec tier classification.

blast_radius.impact: all cross-reference/index files only; no functional callers broken

self_verification.notes: Verified via `git diff -- commands/clean.md`: single line modified at line 589. Pre-existing guards at lines 468 and 479 (checkpoint and cleaner-subagent delegation guards) are untouched. No compile step needed for .md files.

command_enumeration_finding.description: Exhaustive enumeration of all commands/*.md files by ending-workflow adaptation tier. This is deliverable R2 — the answer to the user question 'which other commands also need ending-workflow adaptation'.

tier_A_full_devchain_needed.rationale: dev-command.md is a dev-series command (full BA→dev→QA pipeline) but its ending workflow is weaker than dev.md. User expects identical behavior.

tier_B_push_only_needed.gap: Should note that after the commit, /push is the next step to publish to remote

tier_B_push_only_needed.adaptation: Add one-line note: 'To version-control: commit the file then /push'

out_of_scope_finding.description: The cleaner subagent commits inline (via direct Bash git commit) without writing the push-gate token that /push requires. This means /push will fail after /clean with 'no session commit token found'. The workaround (bash ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh directly) is documented in the clean.md guidance but is an inline bash invocation — contrary to BA spec's 'direction toward next command, not inline git invocations' principle.
out_of_scope_finding.recommended_resolution: Separate dev cycle: have agents/cleaner.md write the canonical push-gate token (same format as changelog-analyst Phase 10) after its inline commit succeeds. This would allow /push to work normally after /clean.

recommendations: Open a follow-up cycle to have agents/cleaner.md write the push-gate token after its inline commit, enabling bare /push usage after /clean
Consider adding /push guidance to commands/dev-command.md (Tier A — HIGH priority) in a separate cycle
Consider adding /push guidance to commands/doc-sync.md (Tier B — MEDIUM priority) in a separate cycle

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Two findings: (1) BLOCKER — /push requires push-gate token which cleaner does not write; advised either adding push-gate write to cleaner or accepting bash/.claude/hooks/push.sh workaround. (2) MAJOR — dev report artifact missing from filesystem at time of review; enumeration was only in ticket/context.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1: Updated clean.md wording to include push-gate caveat and bash push.sh workaround. Set scope_review_requested=true noting the inline-bash-invocation tension with BA spec's 'no inline invocations' constraint. Documented as out_of_scope_finding with recommended_resolution for follow-up cycle. Finding 2: Dev report now written with full enumeration table in command_enumeration_finding section.
